behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real git rename detection semantics and CLI subprocess/output contracts across modules (repo state -> git diff/status output -> parser -> category counter -> stdout report). A unit-only or mocked test can hide failures caused by actual git rename heuristics or subprocess output shape. Rejected higher levels: the behavior text is purely git/CLI logic with no AI/ML call, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, commit baseline files, perform a rename, run the CLI against that repo, and assert the stdout structured report shows the file only in `renamed` (not in `added` or `deleted`).

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary diff handling is tied to real git diff/subprocess behavior and real filesystem content. Mocked text diffs often miss real binary markers/edge cases, creating false confidence. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no LLM/model dependency and no multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and the structured stdout includes the binary change in counts/categories.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly exercises real external error-path behavior from git on an unborn HEAD repository and verifies tool handling via CLI boundary. A fake can miss real exit-code/stderr contract differences. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML behavior in the text.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new real git repo with no commits, run the CLI, and assert successful command behavior with an empty structured report (not an exception/error output).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This requires proving externally visible correctness by round-trip: create real repository history, run CLI, then compare reported counts to actual git history. Unit tests of counting logic alone cannot validate the full contract from real history through subprocess output to final report. Rejected higher levels: no model output quality or agent chain is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known added/deleted/renamed/modified events across commits, run the CLI, independently query git for expected totals, and assert exact match with the tool’s stdout structured counts.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged behavior depends on real git index and working tree semantics, which are OS/repo-state interactions beyond unit scope. Mocking can hide discrepancies in how git reports each state. Rejected higher levels: behavior text does not involve AI/ML calls or multi-step agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, introduce equivalent changes and capture CLI output once with changes unstaged and once staged; assert the structured outputs are identical.
